name: Accordion
summary: Vertical stack of collapsible `Accordion.Item` panels with single or `multiple` open behavior and roving-focus ARIA; control which panels are open with `expanded`/`defaultExpanded`/`onExpandedChange`.

bestPractices: Use for short, scannable sections. Keep headers concise and content lightweight.
Choose single vs multiple expansion by context; avoid opening many sections by default.
Control model deliberately: use `expanded` + `onExpandedChange` for app‑driven state, or `defaultExpanded` for uncontrolled.
Respect heading levels. Set `headingLevel` so the outline remains semantic within the page.
Constrain height in dense pages so the thread scrolls locally rather than the document.
Prefer keeping content mounted unless heavy: use `unmountOnExit` to remove expensive trees when closed.
Ensure keyboard support: Space/Enter toggles, Arrow keys move focus, and focus styles are visible.

Headers are touch-friendly automatically — the full-width header row floors at ≥44px tall on coarse pointers (40px under `compact`), with the mobile chrome kit (no tap-flash, no accidental text selection). Keep header text concise so the row stays a clean tap target.
Each header is wired to its panel via `aria-controls`/`aria-labelledby` with ids namespaced per instance, so multiple Accordions on one page never produce colliding ARIA references.

examples.title: Unmount on exit
examples.code:
<Accordion unmountOnExit>
  <Accordion.Item header='Lazy'>Content mounts only when open</Accordion.Item>
  <Accordion.Item header='Also lazy'>Content mounts only when open</Accordion.Item>
</Accordion>

examples.title: Controlled multiple
